name: clone_up_to_view_variant_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_1_clone_up_to_view_variant_mismatch(msg: CSingleMessage)
    requires
        msg is Message,
        msg@ is Message,
    ensures
        msg.clone_up_to_view()@ is Message,
{
}
reason: `clone_up_to_view` external_body only ensures `c@ == self@` — it guarantees the abstract view matches but not the concrete variant, so the clone could theoretically be an `Ack` or `InvalidMessage` with the same abstract view (though the view function maps different variants to different abstract variants, making this a tautology rather than a real gap)

name: valid_list_empty_any_seqno
code:
proof fn phi_2_valid_list_empty_any_seqno(n: u64, dst: AbstractEndPoint)
    requires
        n as int <= AbstractParameters::static_params().max_seqno,
    ensures
        CAckState::valid_list(Seq::<CSingleMessage>::empty(), n as int, dst),
{
}
reason: With an empty un_acked list, `valid_list` accepts any `num_packets_acked` up to `max_seqno` — there is no constraint linking `num_packets_acked` to actual protocol history, allowing fabricated ack counters

name: un_acked_list_sequential_vacuous_singleton
code:
proof fn phi_3_un_acked_list_sequential_vacuous_singleton(msg: CSingleMessage)
    requires
        msg is Message,
    ensures
        CAckState::un_acked_list_sequential(seq![msg]),
{
}
reason: A singleton un_acked list vacuously satisfies `un_acked_list_sequential` regardless of its seqno value — combined with `valid_list` only checking the first element equals `num_packets_acked + 1`, a single message with any seqno can be made valid by choosing the right `num_packets_acked`

name: valid_ackstate_no_dst_validity
code:
proof fn phi_4_valid_ackstate_no_dst_validity(ack: CAckState, dst: AbstractEndPoint)
    requires
        ack.valid(dst),
        !dst.valid_physical_address(),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: `CAckState::valid` never checks `dst.valid_physical_address()` — a valid ack state can target an endpoint with an arbitrarily long id (>= 0x100000 bytes), which would fail physical address validation elsewhere in the system

name: abstractable_allows_invalid_message_marshalable
code:
proof fn phi_5_abstractable_allows_invalid_message_marshalable(msg: CSingleMessage)
    requires
        msg is InvalidMessage,
    ensures
        msg.abstractable(),
{
}
reason: `InvalidMessage` is unconditionally abstractable — this means invalid/unparseable messages pass the abstractability check and can flow through protocol layers that gate on `abstractable()`, potentially allowing garbage messages to propagate where only well-formed messages should be accepted
